name: run-sql
description: Run SQL queries against PostgreSQL with compact, batch, describe, and list modes, replacing separate psql -c calls with connection flags.

parameters.name: --compact
parameters.description: Compact output: no headers, unaligned, minimal whitespace (uses psql -A -t)

parameters.name: --batch
parameters.description: Run multiple semicolon-separated SQL statements, label each result with a short query preview, uses compact mode automatically (collapses N separate run-sql calls into 1)

parameters.name: --describe
parameters.description: Describe one or more tables (comma-separated) via \d+, collapsing N parallel \d+ calls into 1 step

parameters.name: sql
parameters.description: SQL query string (or pipe via stdin)

examples.call: /app/.preinstalled_scripts/run-sql/main.sh --db=test --batch "SELECT count(*) FROM users; SELECT count(*) FROM alias; SELECT count(*) FROM email_log;"
examples.expected: Count results for each table labeled with query preview

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses psql connection+query chains into one step, saving 2-3 agent steps per database query; --describe collapses N parallel \d+ calls into 1 step, saving N-1 agent steps; --batch collapses N separate run-sql calls into 1 step with labeled compact output, saving N-1 steps.
